channel.title: Transferencia — Spanish Flashcards
channel.description: Per-bundle Spanish flashcard audio drills, voiced by Piper TTS. The 27 bundles (A–Z + Ñ) chunk Mihalis Eleftheriou's 90-lesson Language Transfer Complete Spanish course into thematic groups; each bundle plays as one or more ≤30-minute parts. Three exercise shapes interleave throughout: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills that splice English and Spanish voices then defer the meaning gloss as a built-in second flashcard. Shuffled per bundle, Spanish synthesized slightly slower than English, with a two-tone E-major-7 chime separating cards.
channel.itunes:author: Transferencia

item.title: C1 Helpers Saber
item.description:
Bundle C. Helpers + saber + first I-form — Lessons 8–10. Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: quiere, debo, la/los/las, por qué; saber, sí/si, q-u always /k/, sentence stitching; chop -r add -o; tener + -tain family.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: C2 Helpers Saber
item.description:
Bundle C. Helpers + saber + first I-form — Lessons 8–10. Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: quiere, debo, la/los/las, por qué; saber, sí/si, q-u always /k/, sentence stitching; chop -r add -o; tener + -tain family.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: C3 Helpers Saber
item.description:
Bundle C. Helpers + saber + first I-form — Lessons 8–10. Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: quiere, debo, la/los/las, por qué; saber, sí/si, q-u always /k/, sentence stitching; chop -r add -o; tener + -tain family.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: D1 Present Go Family
item.description:
Bundle D. Regular present + -go family start — Lessons 11–13. Part 1 of 2.
Concepts covered: The two verb groups; you-form -s, they-form -n, deber; full -ar present; -tion → -ción, -ity → -idad; pronoun stacking; hacer/poder/esperar; -go family begins.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: D2 Present Go Family
item.description:
Bundle D. Regular present + -go family start — Lessons 11–13. Part 2 of 2.
Concepts covered: The two verb groups; you-form -s, they-form -n, deber; full -ar present; -tion → -ción, -ity → -idad; pronoun stacking; hacer/poder/esperar; -go family begins.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: E1 Spine Vowel Split
item.description:
Bundle E. Spine roll-call + vowel splits — Lessons 14–17 (4). Part 1 of 2.
Concepts covered: yo/él/ella, sé, pongo, supongo, compongo; spine roll-call (quiero / voy a / tengo que / puedo / debo); e→ie + o→ue split; reflexive me duermo; -amos moves stress (no split).
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: E2 Spine Vowel Split
item.description:
Bundle E. Spine roll-call + vowel splits — Lessons 14–17 (4). Part 2 of 2.
Concepts covered: yo/él/ella, sé, pongo, supongo, compongo; spine roll-call (quiero / voy a / tengo que / puedo / debo); e→ie + o→ue split; reflexive me duermo; -amos moves stress (no split).
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: F1 We Form Future Context
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle F, part 1 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: F2 We Form Future Context
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle F, part 2 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: F3 We Form Future Context
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle F, part 3 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: G1 Haber Past Reflexive
item.description:
Bundle G. Haber-past + reflexive intro — Lessons 21–23. Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: he/has/ha/hemos/han + -ado; -ido for -er/-ir; subject vs object pronouns; -ma masculines (el problema); reflexive past me he perdido.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: G2 Haber Past Reflexive
item.description:
Bundle G. Haber-past + reflexive intro — Lessons 21–23. Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: he/has/ha/hemos/han + -ado; -ido for -er/-ir; subject vs object pronouns; -ma masculines (el problema); reflexive past me he perdido.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: G3 Haber Past Reflexive
item.description:
Bundle G. Haber-past + reflexive intro — Lessons 21–23. Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: he/has/ha/hemos/han + -ado; -ido for -er/-ir; subject vs object pronouns; -ma masculines (el problema); reflexive past me he perdido.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: H1 Indirect Pronouns Dar
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle H, part 1 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: H2 Indirect Pronouns Dar
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle H, part 2 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle H, part 3 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle I, part 1 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: I2 Agreement Ser Estar
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle I, part 2 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle I, part 3 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: J1 Full Ser Flexible Adj
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle J, part 1 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle J, part 2 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: J3 Full Ser Flexible Adj
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle J, part 3 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: K1 Gerund Future
item.description:
Bundle K. Gerund + future tense — Lessons 34–36. Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: -ando/-iendo (this-moment-only); object-pronoun placement on the gerund; estar siendo; salir/salgo; real future tense (-é/-ás/-á/-emos/-án) — haber-endings on the full verb.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: K2 Gerund Future
item.description:
Bundle K. Gerund + future tense — Lessons 34–36. Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: -ando/-iendo (this-moment-only); object-pronoun placement on the gerund; estar siendo; salir/salgo; real future tense (-é/-ás/-á/-emos/-án) — haber-endings on the full verb.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: K3 Gerund Future
item.description:
Bundle K. Gerund + future tense — Lessons 34–36. Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: -ando/-iendo (this-moment-only); object-pronoun placement on the gerund; estar siendo; salir/salgo; real future tense (-é/-ás/-á/-emos/-án) — haber-endings on the full verb.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: L1 Pronoun Migration Se
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle L, part 1 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: L2 Pronoun Migration Se
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle L, part 2 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle L, part 3 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: M1 Conditional Gustar
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle M, part 1 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: M2 Conditional Gustar
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle M, part 2 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: M3 Conditional Gustar
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle M, part 3 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: N1 Gender Exceptions Line Past
item.description:
Bundle N. Gender exceptions + gustar family + line-past intro — Lessons 44–46. Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: la mano, la foto, la moto; interesar/parecer; bien/bueno; poner/-pose; podría/debería practice; three-pasts panorama; line-past -aba for -ar.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: N2 Gender Exceptions Line Past
item.description:
Bundle N. Gender exceptions + gustar family + line-past intro — Lessons 44–46. Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: la mano, la foto, la moto; interesar/parecer; bien/bueno; poner/-pose; podría/debería practice; three-pasts panorama; line-past -aba for -ar.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: N3 Gender Exceptions Line Past
item.description:
Bundle N. Gender exceptions + gustar family + line-past intro — Lessons 44–46. Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: la mano, la foto, la moto; interesar/parecer; bien/bueno; poner/-pose; podría/debería practice; three-pasts panorama; line-past -aba for -ar.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: NN1 Line Past Full
item.description:
Bundle Ñ. Line-past full — Lessons 47–49. Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: -aba expansion + se for "got"; line-past -ía for -er/-ir + se vende; irregular era / iba / veía; llamar/tener line forms (tenía).
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: NN2 Line Past Full
item.description:
Bundle Ñ. Line-past full — Lessons 47–49. Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: -aba expansion + se for "got"; line-past -ía for -er/-ir + se vende; irregular era / iba / veía; llamar/tener line forms (tenía).
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: NN3 Line Past Full
item.description:
Bundle Ñ. Line-past full — Lessons 47–49. Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: -aba expansion + se for "got"; line-past -ía for -er/-ir + se vende; irregular era / iba / veía; llamar/tener line forms (tenía).
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: O1 Para Por Pluperfect
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle O, part 1 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: O2 Para Por Pluperfect
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle O, part 2 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: O3 Para Por Pluperfect
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle O, part 3 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: P1 Irreg Participles Relatives
item.description:
Bundle P. Irregular participles + relatives + -emos/-imos — Lessons 54–57 (4). Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: -ado/-ido as adjectives with estar; irregular past participles roto/dicho/hecho/visto/abierto/cerrado; "prepositions can't end a sentence"; que/cual/quien; -emos vs -imos diff (the one -er vs -ir split that survives in present); lo + adj; queriendo.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: P2 Irreg Participles Relatives
item.description:
Bundle P. Irregular participles + relatives + -emos/-imos — Lessons 54–57 (4). Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: -ado/-ido as adjectives with estar; irregular past participles roto/dicho/hecho/visto/abierto/cerrado; "prepositions can't end a sentence"; que/cual/quien; -emos vs -imos diff (the one -er vs -ir split that survives in present); lo + adj; queriendo.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: P3 Irreg Participles Relatives
item.description:
Bundle P. Irregular participles + relatives + -emos/-imos — Lessons 54–57 (4). Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: -ado/-ido as adjectives with estar; irregular past participles roto/dicho/hecho/visto/abierto/cerrado; "prepositions can't end a sentence"; que/cual/quien; -emos vs -imos diff (the one -er vs -ir split that survives in present); lo + adj; queriendo.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: Q1 Point Past
item.description:
Bundle Q. Point-past intro — Lessons 58–60. Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: Point-past -ar (-é / -ó) with critical accents; passive se envió; point-past -er/-ir (-í / -ió); double negatives; "we" point-past anomaly (-er differs: comemos vs comimos).
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: Q2 Point Past
item.description:
Bundle Q. Point-past intro — Lessons 58–60. Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: Point-past -ar (-é / -ó) with critical accents; passive se envió; point-past -er/-ir (-í / -ió); double negatives; "we" point-past anomaly (-er differs: comemos vs comimos).
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: Q3 Point Past
item.description:
Bundle Q. Point-past intro — Lessons 58–60. Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: Point-past -ar (-é / -ó) with critical accents; passive se envió; point-past -er/-ir (-í / -ió); double negatives; "we" point-past anomaly (-er differs: comemos vs comimos).
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: R1 Past Context Dar
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle R, part 1 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: R2 Past Context Dar
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle R, part 2 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: R3 Past Context Dar
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle R, part 3 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: S1 Hace Possessives Accents
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle S, part 1 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: S2 Hace Possessives Accents
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle S, part 2 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: S3 Hace Possessives Accents
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle S, part 3 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: T1 Demonstratives Mood
item.description:
Bundle T. Demonstratives + mood-tense intro — Lessons 68–70. Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: este/ese/esto family (incl. neuter); día masc; tan; creer; cuántas veces; mood tense intro — take I-form, swap groups; obligatory after quiero que; lo que; necesito que / hace falta que; faltar/falta.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: T2 Demonstratives Mood
item.description:
Bundle T. Demonstratives + mood-tense intro — Lessons 68–70. Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: este/ese/esto family (incl. neuter); día masc; tan; creer; cuántas veces; mood tense intro — take I-form, swap groups; obligatory after quiero que; lo que; necesito que / hace falta que; faltar/falta.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: T3 Demonstratives Mood
item.description:
Bundle T. Demonstratives + mood-tense intro — Lessons 68–70. Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: este/ese/esto family (incl. neuter); día masc; tan; creer; cuántas veces; mood tense intro — take I-form, swap groups; obligatory after quiero que; lo que; necesito que / hace falta que; faltar/falta.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: U1 Mood Commands
item.description:
Bundle U. Mood triggers + commands — Lessons 71–73. Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: espero que / me gusta que; contar / contar con; mood-tense of ver (vea/vean); personal a; a + el = al; commands (mood for ALL negatives, formal positive, plural positive, let's); informal-positive shortcut = drop -s from tú form; little words attach to END of positive commands; cuando + future event; ir → vaya.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: U2 Mood Commands
item.description:
Bundle U. Mood triggers + commands — Lessons 71–73. Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: espero que / me gusta que; contar / contar con; mood-tense of ver (vea/vean); personal a; a + el = al; commands (mood for ALL negatives, formal positive, plural positive, let's); informal-positive shortcut = drop -s from tú form; little words attach to END of positive commands; cuando + future event; ir → vaya.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: U3 Mood Commands
item.description:
Bundle U. Mood triggers + commands — Lessons 71–73. Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: espero que / me gusta que; contar / contar con; mood-tense of ver (vea/vean); personal a; a + el = al; commands (mood for ALL negatives, formal positive, plural positive, let's); informal-positive shortcut = drop -s from tú form; little words attach to END of positive commands; cuando + future event; ir → vaya.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: V1 Mood Expansion
item.description:
Bundle V. Mood expansion — Lessons 74–77 (4). Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: elegir (e→i, the third pattern); para que + mood; irregular hecho after haber; también breakdown; donde/como/que/cuando + mood = wherever/however/whatever/whenever; hacer command haz; seguir family (e→i); sea; hay/había + mood; tiene que haber; es + adj + que + mood; qué fuerte que….
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: V2 Mood Expansion
item.description:
Bundle V. Mood expansion — Lessons 74–77 (4). Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: elegir (e→i, the third pattern); para que + mood; irregular hecho after haber; también breakdown; donde/como/que/cuando + mood = wherever/however/whatever/whenever; hacer command haz; seguir family (e→i); sea; hay/había + mood; tiene que haber; es + adj + que + mood; qué fuerte que….
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: V3 Mood Expansion
item.description:
Bundle V. Mood expansion — Lessons 74–77 (4). Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: elegir (e→i, the third pattern); para que + mood; irregular hecho after haber; también breakdown; donde/como/que/cuando + mood = wherever/however/whatever/whenever; hacer command haz; seguir family (e→i); sea; hay/había + mood; tiene que haber; es + adj + que + mood; qué fuerte que….
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: W1 Seguir Se Uses
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle W, part 1 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: W2 Seguir Se Uses
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle W, part 2 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: W3 Seguir Se Uses
item.description: Spanish flashcard drill — bundle W, part 3 of 3. Three exercise shapes interleave: English → Spanish translation, Spanish → English translation, and conjugation drills with mixed English / Spanish text-to-speech voicing.

item.title: X1 Past Mood
item.description:
Bundle X. Past mood tense — Lessons 81–83. Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: se me + mood; volver a = "again"; PAST MOOD TENSE (hablaran/hablara/habláramos); quería que / esperaba que / necesitábamos que; mood after ser/estar + adj + que; the lo + adj + es que trick to AVOID the mood; era (line-past of ser); past mood after que and after si (hypothetical past); si pudiera… lo cancelaría; alt -ase/-iese form.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: X2 Past Mood
item.description:
Bundle X. Past mood tense — Lessons 81–83. Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: se me + mood; volver a = "again"; PAST MOOD TENSE (hablaran/hablara/habláramos); quería que / esperaba que / necesitábamos que; mood after ser/estar + adj + que; the lo + adj + es que trick to AVOID the mood; era (line-past of ser); past mood after que and after si (hypothetical past); si pudiera… lo cancelaría; alt -ase/-iese form.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: X3 Past Mood
item.description:
Bundle X. Past mood tense — Lessons 81–83. Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: se me + mood; volver a = "again"; PAST MOOD TENSE (hablaran/hablara/habláramos); quería que / esperaba que / necesitábamos que; mood after ser/estar + adj + que; the lo + adj + es que trick to AVOID the mood; era (line-past of ser); past mood after que and after si (hypothetical past); si pudiera… lo cancelaría; alt -ase/-iese form.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: Y1 Go Verbs Future Irreg
item.description:
Bundle Y. Go-verbs + future contractions + irreg point-past start — Lessons 84–87 (4). Part 1 of 2.
Concepts covered: Full "go verbs" family (tengo, vengo, salgo, pongo, digo, hago, oigo, traigo); mood swaps -go → -ga; go-verb commands (formal -ga; informal positive = drop -go: ten, sal, ven, di, pon); future contractions (tendré, saldré, vendré, pondré, haré, diré, podré); line-past irregulars + irregular point-past family (tuve, estuve, pude, hube — root change, mixed -ar/-er-ir endings, no accent).
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: Y2 Go Verbs Future Irreg
item.description:
Bundle Y. Go-verbs + future contractions + irreg point-past start — Lessons 84–87 (4). Part 2 of 2.
Concepts covered: Full "go verbs" family (tengo, vengo, salgo, pongo, digo, hago, oigo, traigo); mood swaps -go → -ga; go-verb commands (formal -ga; informal positive = drop -go: ten, sal, ven, di, pon); future contractions (tendré, saldré, vendré, pondré, haré, diré, podré); line-past irregulars + irregular point-past family (tuve, estuve, pude, hube — root change, mixed -ar/-er-ir endings, no accent).
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: Z1 Closeout Dialects
item.description:
Bundle Z. Closeout + dialects — Lessons 88–90. Part 1 of 3.
Concepts covered: More irregular point-past (puse, hice/hizo, vine, traje, fui); fui shared for ser AND ir; spelling switch c→z in hizo; dropped i in fueron/dijeron; building past mood from the they-form of point-past; rule-discovery puzzle (short feminine a-…-a takes el, uno/alguno/ninguno → un/algún/ningún before masc, demasiado changes with nouns but not adjectives); regional accents, leísmo, vosotros, vos, aspirated s; closing pep talk on listening.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: Z2 Closeout Dialects
item.description:
Bundle Z. Closeout + dialects — Lessons 88–90. Part 2 of 3.
Concepts covered: More irregular point-past (puse, hice/hizo, vine, traje, fui); fui shared for ser AND ir; spelling switch c→z in hizo; dropped i in fueron/dijeron; building past mood from the they-form of point-past; rule-discovery puzzle (short feminine a-…-a takes el, uno/alguno/ninguno → un/algún/ningún before masc, demasiado changes with nouns but not adjectives); regional accents, leísmo, vosotros, vos, aspirated s; closing pep talk on listening.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.

item.title: Z3 Closeout Dialects
item.description:
Bundle Z. Closeout + dialects — Lessons 88–90. Part 3 of 3.
Concepts covered: More irregular point-past (puse, hice/hizo, vine, traje, fui); fui shared for ser AND ir; spelling switch c→z in hizo; dropped i in fueron/dijeron; building past mood from the they-form of point-past; rule-discovery puzzle (short feminine a-…-a takes el, uno/alguno/ninguno → un/algún/ningún before masc, demasiado changes with nouns but not adjectives); regional accents, leísmo, vosotros, vos, aspirated s; closing pep talk on listening.
Three exercise shapes interleave throughout this part: • English → Spanish translation — produce the Spanish for the English prompt. • Spanish → English translation — sentences are spoken twice (with a long pause between) so you can catch them on the second pass. • Conjugation drills — the prompt splices an English instruction with the Spanish infinitive (e.g. 'Conjugate the I form for' + 'dormir'). The answer plays the conjugated form, pauses, then restates it with the English meaning as a built-in second flashcard for the gloss.
Spanish synthesis runs slightly slower than English so words don't mash. A two-tone E-major-7 chime separates cards.
